generated_oml:
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "100W heating element for thermal regulation, interfaced via Raspberry Pi GPIO"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator for airflow control linked to thermal management of incubator"
]

instance DS18S20TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensors with -55°C to +125°C range, ±0.5°C accuracy deployed across incubator zones"
]

instance SensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20TemperatureSensor
]

instance TemperatureMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from DS18S20 sensors via GPIO, sampled via 1-Wire bus"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataStream
]

instance DifferentialEquationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic models described by differential equations for force dynamics and motion prediction in physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ForceFieldSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationAlgorithm
]

instance CADGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static CAD models representing wheel sizes, chassis shapes, and structural attributes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEngine
]

instance ForceFieldSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Environmental interaction simulations modeling aerodynamic drag and road conditions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationModel
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server on Raspberry Pi facilitating real-time bidirectional data synchronization between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Single-board computer handling GPIO interfaces, data preprocessing, and sensor coordination"
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataPreprocessing
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuatorControlInterface
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated techniques refining model parameters using real-time sensor data for CPS/environmental models"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance StateEstimationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms reconstructing operational state from noisy sensor data for fault detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalysisService
]

instance FaultDetectionSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data-driven techniques identifying anomalies in system performance"
    DTDFVocab:enables ViolationDetectionService
    DTDFVocab:enables SupervisoryControlService
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data transmission pipeline from sensors to RabbitMQ via GPIO"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous model parameter refinement using sensor-derived data for CPS/environmental models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithmic checks detecting deviations (e.g., overheating) and triggering corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationAction
]

instance PerformanceMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring system assessing system performance (safety, optimality) via state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalPerformanceInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavior prediction under hypothetical conditions using differential equation models"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveForceAnalysis
]

instance ViolationDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identifies suboptimal/safety-critical conditions via fault detection algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:provides ViolationDetection
]

instance SupervisoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated corrective actions for PT adjustments based on DT-generated predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedAdjustmentAction
]

instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings informing thermal regulation and safety monitoring"
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calibration accuracy metrics quantifying model-physical twin alignment via parameter estimates"
]

instance SafetyViolationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated intervention when overheating/safety thresholds are exceeded (embedded controller enforcement)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics (safety/optimality) derived from state estimation and monitoring algorithms"
]

instance PredictiveForceAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Force-induced movement predictions under current and hypothetical conditions via differential equation models"
]

instance ViolationDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations from optimal/safe operating conditions via fault detection algorithms"
]

instance AutomatedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Parameter adjustments to chassis design/sensor placements via optimizers for efficiency improvements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StructuralAnalysisEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine analyzing structural integrity and performance metrics of CAD geometry models"
]

instance SensorDataPreprocessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing pipeline for raw sensor data from DS18S20 temperature sensors"
]

instance ActuatorControlInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interface managing GPIO communication for actuator control"
]

instance PhysicalCarEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DifferentialEquationModel,
    CADGeometryModel,
    ForceFieldSimulation
]

instance PhysicalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator,
    FanActuator,
    DS18S20TemperatureSensor
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) synchronizes data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) through sensors over a **temporal granularity of 'over time'**, implying periodic or event-driven data acquisition rather than continuous real-time streams. Calibration algorithms periodically update the DT models with the best parameter estimates derived from the collected data, ensuring alignment between the digital and physical representations. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies, the workflow involves a **closed-loop feedback mechanism** where monitors evaluate system performance and trigger interventions (human or algorithmic) upon detected violations, suggesting a **discrete, event-based temporal coordination** rather than continuous synchronization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system described in the documents follows a **centralized architecture pattern**, where the DT integrates **models, data streams from sensors**, and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators, optimizers) to simulate and optimize the behavior of the Physical Twin (PT). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models dynamically with real-time data, ensuring alignment with the PT’s operational parameters. The system-wide coordination is achieved through a **closed-loop feedback mechanism**: data from sensors informs monitors, which detect deviations from optimal performance. Violations trigger interventions (human or algorithmic) to adjust the PT. The DT’s structure is depicted as a cohesive unit in the implied figure (Fig. 1), emphasizing **resource management** via centralized control of model updates and monitoring workflows. No distributed orchestration patterns or specific integration tools (e.g., middleware, APIs) are mentioned, but the DT’s core relies on **real-time synchronization** and **algorithm-driven decision-making** for seamless operation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) in the provided context is implicitly defined through two key phases:
1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Representation Types**: Initially, models are created to represent specific aspects of the system, such as static models (e.g., CAD models for physical dimensions like wheel sizes, chassis shapes, or car styles) and dynamic models (e.g., differential equation-based models for dynamics like force-driven movement).
   - **Transition Management**: Once the system is deployed, the static and dynamic models are integrated into the DT to ensure continuous alignment with the Physical Twin (PT). This involves updating models with real-time data from sensors and refining them through calibration algorithms (e.g., non-linear least squares) to match operational conditions.

2. **Operational Phase**:
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: The DT remains active post-deployment, incorporating real-time data, model refinements, and algorithmic interventions (e.g., fault detection, state estimation) to sustain value. There is no explicit mention of formalized lifecycle stages like 'Conceptualization & Design' or 'Integration & Deployment,' but the integration of models into the DT after deployment is emphasized as a continuous process.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validation**:
  The fidelity of DT models is critical for accurate predictions and operational decisions. The provided documents emphasize that models must be **calibrated dynamically** using real-time data from the PT to ensure alignment with physical behavior. Key considerations include:
  - **Validation Methods**: Models are validated through **simulation-to-reality comparisons**, where predictions from the DT are cross-checked against observed PT performance under identical conditions.
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: While not explicitly quantified in the documents, implicit reliance on **algorithmic calibration** (e.g., via iterative parameter updates) suggests that uncertainty is managed through iterative refinement of model parameters.
  - **Error Detection/Correction**: The DT employs **monitoring systems** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to detect deviations between predicted and actual PT behavior. Violations trigger **automated or human-driven corrections** to models or PT operations.

  **Quality Assurance Processes**:
  The integration of models into the DT framework ensures **long-term maintenance** rather than discard, with continuous validation loops. The documents imply that fidelity is preserved through:
  - **Data-Driven Model Updates**: Sensors provide real-time feedback to adjust models dynamically.
  - **Safety-Critical Validation**: Models must pass rigorous checks to ensure they do not introduce risks (e.g., incorrect predictions leading to unsafe PT states).
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on a **local edge infrastructure** hosted on a **Raspberry Pi** with built-in **Wi-Fi and Bluetooth** capabilities. Communication is mediated by **RabbitMQ**, a lightweight message broker deployed on the same device for low-latency synchronization. The network topology is **single-node** (Raspberry Pi as both host and broker), with no explicit mention of redundancy or failover mechanisms. Bandwidth and latency requirements are implicitly optimized for real-time sensor data streams (e.g., temperature updates) and control commands, leveraging the Pi’s embedded processing. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the RabbitMQ broker’s default security measures (e.g., TLS for encrypted communication) are assumed to protect data integrity during transmission.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting deployment** is implemented as an **on-premise/edge deployment model**, with the entire system (RabbitMQ server, Raspberry Pi, and sensor nodes) co-located on a single device. The computational requirements are met by the **Raspberry Pi’s embedded CPU**, which supports real-time processing of sensor data and control logic. Storage is managed via the Pi’s onboard SSD or external interfaces, though specific capacity requirements are not quantified. Scalability mechanisms are limited to the edge deployment, with no mention of cloud-based expansion. Availability/reliability is ensured through the Raspberry Pi’s hardware redundancy (e.g., dual-core ARM Cortex-A7) and the RabbitMQ broker’s built-in resilience to message loss, though no explicit uptime guarantees are provided.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "A Digital Twin (DT) system incorporates **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with real-time sensor data to ensure accurate parameter estimation. The DT employs **monitoring systems** that verify whether the system operates safely and optimally by detecting deviations from expected behavior. While the document does not explicitly mention authentication systems or access control mechanisms, it implies a **safe protocol** in the low-level driver to maintain the device in a controlled, fail-safe state. The DT structure relies on algorithms that detect anomalies (e.g., faults, suboptimal performance) and trigger corrective actions (e.g., human intervention or automated adjustments) to mitigate risks. No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection) or formal risk assessment methodologies is provided, but the integration of monitoring and calibration aligns with safety-critical system considerations."
]
